---
title: "Session 4 · ANCOVA & Statistical Control"
---

## Overview

This session combines a categorical predictor with a continuous covariate, giving us the analysis of covariance (ANCOVA). The payoff is the idea of statistical control: by holding a covariate constant, we can ask whether a group effect survives adjustment --- and watch a difference shrink, vanish, or even reverse once a confounder enters the model. This is a pivotal step in the course's move from simple comparisons toward genuine multiple regression.

## Learning objectives

By the end of this session, you will be able to:

- Specify an ANCOVA model with one categorical predictor and one continuous covariate.
- Explain what it means to "control for" or "adjust for" a covariate.
- Compare unadjusted and adjusted group effects and interpret the difference.
- Recognize confounding and describe how adding a covariate can change a group effect.
- Fit and interpret an ANCOVA model in R.

## This session's materials

- {{< fa book-open-reader >}} **Read:** [Chapter 2: ANCOVA](/notes/chapters/02-ancova.html){target="_blank"}
- {{< fa laptop-code >}} **Lab:** [Lab 3: ANCOVA](/labs/labs/lab03-anova-ancova.html){target="_blank"}
- {{< fa pencil >}} **Homework due:** [Homework 2: Dummy Variables](/assignment/hw02.html)
- {{< fa circle-question >}} **Practice:** [Practice Quiz 2](/practice/chapters/quiz2-dummy-variables.html){target="_blank"}
- {{< fa circle-check >}} **Graded quiz:** [Module Quiz 2](/assignment/quizzes.html#quiz-2)

## Key concepts

| Concept | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| **ANCOVA** | A model combining a categorical predictor with a continuous covariate. |
| **Covariate** | A continuous variable included to adjust the estimated group effect. |
| **Statistical control** | Isolating a predictor's effect by holding other variables constant. |
| **Adjustment** | Re-estimating a group effect after accounting for a covariate. |
| **Adjusted group effect** | The group difference that remains once the covariate is held fixed. |
| **Confounding** | When a third variable distorts the apparent relationship between predictor and outcome. |
| **Parallel slopes** | The ANCOVA assumption that the covariate's effect is the same across groups. |
